pi_items.source: Calculated directly from the synthetically generated CSV using the DOT standard (388 on-time out of 500 flights)
Calculated directly from the synthetically generated CSV (13 cancellations out of 500 flights)
Empirical calculation of OTP per route shows MFR-PDX at 66.67% and SEA-BOI at 69.01%
Empirical calculation of OTP shows GEG-SEA tied with SFO-PDX at 86.11%
Columns corresponding to FAA delay categories in the generated CSV
Flights are generated sequentially between 2024-07-01 and 2024-09-30

privileged_info_summary: The data for this task is dynamically generated via a deterministic Python script. The expected overall OTP percentage is exactly 77.6%, contradicting the ~78% metadata claim. The empirical cancellation rate is exactly 2.6% (13 out of 500 flights), contradicting the 3.2% metadata claim which incorrectly assumed the expected probability over the actual sample count. Using the DOT standard OTP (counting cancelled flights as late), the worst performing route is MFR-PDX at 66.67% OTP, contradicting the prompt's assumption that SEA-BOI (69.01% OTP) is the worst. The best performing routes are tied between GEG-SEA and SFO-PDX at 86.11% OTP. The delay categories (Carrier, Weather, NAS, Security, Late Aircraft) and reporting months (July, August, September) align exactly with the provided metadata.
